message:
fix(edges): plumb owning Graph through to EdgeDataView via _EDGE_VIEW_GRAPH_OWNER (br-r37-c1-dc14n)

Follow-on to f72fb3f. The adj-walk path added in that commit only
fires when EdgeDataView is constructed with `graph=...`, but the
Rust-side EdgeView property factory in _private_aware_edges does not
have a clean way to inject the owning graph at lazy-view construction
time — EdgeDataView is created later by the user calling view(nbunch=...).

Bridge the gap with a per-process id(EdgeView) -> Graph dict
populated each time the @property edges() runs:

  - _private_aware_edges.edges() registers
    _EDGE_VIEW_GRAPH_OWNER[id(view)] = self on every access.
  - EdgeDataView.__init__ falls back to looking up
    _EDGE_VIEW_GRAPH_OWNER.get(id(view_self)) when its `graph`
    constructor argument is None.
  - The lookup is wrapped in try/except (NameError, TypeError) so
    the call still works during module import (before
    _EDGE_VIEW_GRAPH_OWNER is bound) and for views whose id() is
    unhashable for any reason — in those edge cases we silently fall
    through to the original Rust code path.
  - Entries are overwritten on every @property access so stale ids
    from garbage-collected views are self-flushing; we deliberately
    do not bother with weakref because the dict is rewritten on the
    next access anyway.

Pure plumbing — no behavioral change to any other code path; the
adj-walk path from f72fb3f now actually fires under normal usage
instead of only when callers manually pass `graph=...` to
EdgeDataView.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -223,6 +223,15 @@ def __init__(self, edge_view_call, view_self, *, data, default, nbunch_list, gra
         self._default = default
         self._nbunch_list = nbunch_list
         self._nbset = set(nbunch_list) if nbunch_list is not None else None
+        # br-r37-c1-dc14n: graph reference (when known) lets the
+        # adj-walk path compute edges in nx-matching adj order. Look
+        # up via the global EdgeView->Graph map populated by the
+        # _private_aware_edges property if not provided.
+        if graph is None:
+            try:
+                graph = _EDGE_VIEW_GRAPH_OWNER.get(id(view_self))
+            except (NameError, TypeError):
+                graph = None
         self._graph = graph
 
     def _materialize(self):
@@ -22551,11 +22560,29 @@ def _private_aware_edges(raw_edges):
     def edges(self):
         if _has_networkx_private_storage(self):
             return _AssignedPrivateEdgeView(self)
-        return raw_edges.__get__(self, type(self))
+        view = raw_edges.__get__(self, type(self))
+        # br-r37-c1-dc14n: register the graph in the per-process
+        # weakref map so EdgeDataView (created lazily when the user
+        # calls view(nbunch=...)) can recover the owning graph and
+        # iterate adj in nx-matching order. Keyed by id(view); the
+        # entry is overwritten each access.
+        try:
+            _EDGE_VIEW_GRAPH_OWNER[id(view)] = self
+        except TypeError:
+            # If the view is unhashable for some reason, fall through —
+            # EdgeDataView will skip the adj-walk and return raw output.
+            pass
+        return view
 
     return property(edges)
 
 
+# id(EdgeView) -> owning Graph (weak via plain dict; entries are
+# refreshed on every property access so stale ids self-flush). Used
+# by EdgeDataView._materialize_via_adj_walk for br-r37-c1-dc14n.
+_EDGE_VIEW_GRAPH_OWNER = {}
+
+
 def _private_aware_degree(raw_degree):
     def degree(self):
         if _has_networkx_private_storage(self):
